title: CRUD CLI parity testing for tsk/tskr vs beads
description: Establish a temporary test repository under tmp/test-project with Beads initialized to validate CRUD commands. Document one-to-one mappings between tsk/tskr CLI commands and their beads equivalents and note gaps. Polish behaviors and tests as needed.
creator: Ryan Alyn Porter

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Created tmp/test-project as isolated git repo and copied .beads from Taskulus (excluding bd.sock/lock/pid) for CLI parity testing.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Initialized tmp/test-project as standalone git repo, copied .beads (excluding socket/lock/pid), updated repo ID with bd migrate --update-repo-id, imported JSONL, and removed redirect file so bd commands run cleanly there.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text:
Ran CLI parity smoke in tmp/test-project:
- bd create -> tskl-aws (stored in .beads; bd show renders Beads formatting).
- tsk (python) --beads create -> tsk-f33794 stored in project/issues/*.json (not visible to bd).
- tskr (rust) --beads create -> tsk-9fbf53 stored in project/issues/*.json (not visible to bd).
Used project-local init for tsk/tskr; outputs captured for comparison.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text:
Ran CLI parity smoke in tmp/test-project:
- bd create -> tskl-aws (stored in .beads; bd show renders Beads formatting).
- tsk (python) --beads create -> kanbus-f33794 stored in project/issues/*.json (not visible to bd).
- tskr (rust) --beads create -> kanbus-9fbf53 stored in project/issues/*.json (not visible to bd).
Used project-local init for tsk/tskr; outputs captured for comparison.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Patched rust cucumber runner to avoid canonicalize failure on features path; cargo test (rust) now passes. Cleaned tmp/test-project and pushed commit 0654d6e to main after bd sync.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text: Ran Python behave suite under .venv (Python 3.11) with coverage collection. All 34 features passed (272 scenarios, 0 failed). Coverage when measuring all code showed 99% overall including step defs; source-only report (coverage run --source=src) currently ~72% (many step-driven paths not fully covered). No code changes after tests; working tree clean.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text:
Recreated tmp/test-project with copied .beads, added .taskulus.yml to include event type. Ran CRUD smoke:
- tsk create -> tsk-29cf7a; tskr create -> tsk-56a696; bd create -> tskl-y7j.
- Updated each to in_progress with descriptions using respective CLIs.
- Verified show outputs for all three; list works now in tsk/tskr with Beads data after config and repo-id migration. tmp/test-project left in place for further CRUD comparisons.

comments.author: Ryan Alyn Porter
comments.text:
Recreated tmp/test-project with copied .beads, added .taskulus.yml to include event type. Ran CRUD smoke:
- tsk create -> kanbus-29cf7a; tskr create -> kanbus-56a696; bd create -> tskl-y7j.
- Updated each to in_progress with descriptions using respective CLIs.
- Verified show outputs for all three; list works now in tsk/tskr with Beads data after config and repo-id migration. tmp/test-project left in place for further CRUD comparisons.

custom.beads_acceptance_criteria:
1) Temporary repo tmp/test-project exists with Beads initialized using copied .beads state.
2) Mappings documented for each CRUD-related tsk/tskr command to beads equivalents, including any discrepancies.
3) Scenarios/tests added or updated to cover CRUD CLI behaviors using the test repo.
4) Epic status updated with results and follow-ups.
